---
title: "Differential adundance & expression edgeR Analyses"
author: "lily yang"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---
# set up enrivonment and load data
```{r, setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

work_dir <- Sys.getenv("work_dir")
bsp_num <- Sys.getenv("bsp_num")
clustK <- Sys.getenv("clustK")
sampSet <- Sys.getenv("sampSet")
current_run <- sampSet


knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = work_dir)


if (clustK == "") {
   clustK <- 20          # defaulty
}
print(clustK)

print(paste('work_dir', work_dir, 'sampSet', sampSet, bsp_num))              
if (sampSet == "") {
   sampSet <- "error, sampSet not provided"

   }
print(sampSet)
pfx <- paste0(work_dir,"Results/")
dir.create(pfx, showWarnings = FALSE)

```


This script is usually run on the hpc, see "lily code/html/html_differential.sh".

```{r load input data }


# sce_all <- readRDS(paste0('sce_',sampSet,'_initial_cluster.rds')) 
# sce_all_corrected <- readRDS(paste0('sce_',sampSet,'_corrected_initial_cluster.rds'))   


# if we refine initial cluster results by conduct sub-clustering or re-clustering
sce_all <- readRDS(paste0('sce_',sampSet,'_with_refined_cluster.rds'))
sce_all_corrected <- readRDS(paste0('sce_',sampSet,'_corrected_with_refined_cluster.rds'))
  
unique(sce_all_corrected$cluster)
unique(sce_all$cluster)
# sce_all_corrected$cluster <- sce_all$cluster

```


This script picks up where "cluster_lily.Rmd" left off. 

We will start by labelling the clusters 
We will then run differential analyses between conditions. 

Specifically, we will be looking for differential abundance of 'cells within each cluster', as well as differential expression of 'cells within each cluster', according to treatment condition. 

There are 2 conditions: virus1 vs virus1 vs control, 2 time point: DPI 1 vs DPI 12



# Loading the packages

```{r load packages}

library(SingleCellExperiment)
library(DropletUtils)
library(edgeR)
library(bluster)
library(scran)
library(scater)
library(uwot)
library(biomaRt)
library(scds)
library(batchelor)
library(gridExtra)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)

```



# Cluster annotation, remove clusters from downstream analysis 

Based on manual annotation from the clustered data using the generated marker gene tables and our shiny app, we'll give the clusters more intuitive names that can be transposed into figures. As a quick check in this script, we look at particular marker genes first.

```{r double check by look at marker genes expression used in cell sorting}
## first look at marker genes for sorting



# plotExpression(sce_all_corrected, features=rownames(sce_all)[rowData(sce_all)$hgnc_symbol == 'CD4'], x='cluster', exprs_values='logcounts')
plotExpression(sce_all_corrected, features=rownames(sce_all_corrected)[rowData(sce_all_corrected)$Symbol == 'CD4'], x='cluster')

# plotExpression(sce_all_corrected, features=rownames(sce_all)[rowData(sce_all)$Symbol == 'CD8B'], x='cluster', exprs_values='logcounts')
plotExpression(sce_all_corrected, features=rownames(sce_all_corrected)[rowData(sce_all_corrected)$Symbol == 'CD8B'], x='cluster')

# plotExpression(sce_all_corrected, features=rownames(sce_all)[rowData(sce_all)$Symbol == 'CD163'], x='cluster', exprs_values='logcounts')
plotExpression(sce_all_corrected, features=rownames(sce_all_corrected)[rowData(sce_all_corrected)$Symbol == 'CD163'], x='cluster')

# plotExpression(sce_all_corrected, features=rownames(sce_all)[rowData(sce_all)$Symbol == 'CD14'], x='cluster', exprs_values='logcounts')
plotExpression(sce_all_corrected, features=rownames(sce_all_corrected)[rowData(sce_all_corrected)$Symbol == 'CD14'], x='cluster')
 
# plotExpression(sce_all_corrected, features=rownames(sce_all)[rowData(sce_all)$Symbol == 'CD3D'], x='cluster', exprs_values='logcounts')
plotExpression(sce_all_corrected, features=rownames(sce_all_corrected)[rowData(sce_all_corrected)$Symbol == 'CD3D'], x='cluster')

# plotExpression(sce_all_corrected, features=rownames(sce_all)[rowData(sce_all)$Symbol == 'CD3E'], x='cluster', exprs_values='logcounts')
plotExpression(sce_all_corrected, features=rownames(sce_all_corrected)[rowData(sce_all_corrected)$Symbol == 'CD3E'], x='cluster')

```



Note that we are maintaining 2 separate datasets here: 
sce_filt (from sce_all_corrected), which contains all detected genes and reconstructed gene expression values after sequencing run batch correction

sce_all_filt (from sce_all), which contains all detected genes with normalised (but not batch corrected) log-count values and original counts. 

sce_all_filt is important for count-based differential analyses and seeing expression values that aren't interpolated after batch correction.


Next we name the good quality clusters. 

```{r name cell types for each cluster }
sce_filt <- sce_all_corrected
sce_all_filt <- sce_all
unique(sce_all$cluster)

print(sce_all)
# rm(sce_all, sce_all_corrected)

## add in cluster names as sce_all_filt$named_cluster
#Long-form annotated version including cell subtypes
if(sampSet == "subset_innate_immune_cells")
{
  munual_annotation <- read.csv("munual_annotation_innate_immune_cells.csv", header = FALSE)  
}else if(sampSet == "subset_adaptive_immune_cells")
{
  munual_annotation <- read.csv("munual_annotation_adaptive_immune_cells.csv", header = FALSE)
}else if(sampSet == "subset_non_immune_cells")
{
  munual_annotation <- read.csv("munual_annotation_non_immune_cells.csv", header = FALSE)
}else
{
  munual_annotation <- read.csv("munual_annotation.csv", header = FALSE) 
}


colnames(munual_annotation) <- c("cluster", "name")
# if(sampSet == "subset_adaptive_immune_cells")
# { 
#   # remove the content in parentheses as well as the parentheses
#   munual_annotation$name <- sub(" \\(.*\\)", "", munual_annotation$name)
#   
# }

# add an column show all clusters belong to same cell types
library(dplyr)
df_grouped <- munual_annotation %>%
  dplyr::group_by(name) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(cluster_contained = paste(cluster, collapse = ", ")) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()
df_grouped$celltype_cluster <- paste(df_grouped$name, df_grouped$cluster_contained)

unique(sce_all_filt$cluster)
sce_all_filt$cluster <- as.character(sce_all_filt$cluster)


if(bsp_num == "bsp_248")
{
  for (i in unique(sce_all_filt$cluster))
  {
    cell_type <- munual_annotation[ munual_annotation$cluster == i, "name"]
    long_celltype <- as.character(df_grouped[ df_grouped$name == cell_type, "celltype_cluster"])         
    # print(paste(i, cell_type, long_celltype))
    # print(typeof(long_celltype))
    sce_all_filt$named_cluster[sce_all_filt$cluster==i] <- cell_type
    sce_all_filt$long_named_cluster[sce_all_filt$cluster==i] <- long_celltype
  }
}
table(sce_all_filt$long_named_cluster)
# head(sce_all_filt$long_named_cluster)
# sce_all_filt$cluster <- as.integer(sce_all_filt$cluster)

unique(sce_all_filt$long_named_cluster)


```


```{r manually assign color to the good quality clusters}
# Create label and assign colour for each cluster for convenient annotation later

# label every cell with its tissue type and the cluster it belong to
sce_all_filt$verbose_cluster <- paste0('lung, ', sce_all_filt$named_cluster, ', ', sce_all_filt$cluster)
# label every cell with its tissue type 
sce_all_filt$EXP_celltype <- paste0('lung, ', sce_all_filt$named_cluster)

sce_all_filt

# BAL_colour_list <- vector("list", length(sce_all_filt$EXP_celltype))
# BAL_colour_list[grep('Macrophages', sce_all_filt$EXP_celltype)] <- '#E31A1C' #red
# BAL_colour_list[grep('Monocytes', sce_all_filt$EXP_celltype)] <- '#C9C32F' #pink
# BAL_colour_list[grep('conventional', sce_all_filt$EXP_celltype)] <- '#FB9A99' 
# BAL_colour_list[grep('pDCs', sce_all_filt$EXP_celltype)] <- '#FDDBC7' 
# BAL_colour_list[grep('cDCs', sce_all_filt$EXP_celltype)] <- '#6A3D9A' #purple
# BAL_colour_list[grep('Plasma Cells', sce_all_filt$EXP_celltype)] <- '#1F78B4' #dark blue
# BAL_colour_list[grep('B Cells', sce_all_filt$EXP_celltype)] <- '#A6CEE3' #pale blue
# BAL_colour_list[grep('Tregs', sce_all_filt$EXP_celltype)] <- '#CAB2D6' #light purple
# BAL_colour_list[grep('CD4', sce_all_filt$EXP_celltype)] <- '#B2DF8A' #light green
# BAL_colour_list[grep('Mitotic CD4 T Cells', sce_all_filt$EXP_celltype)] <- '#FFFF00' 
# BAL_colour_list[grep('CD8', sce_all_filt$EXP_celltype)] <- '#33A02C' # green
# BAL_colour_list[grep('Mitotic CD8 T Cells', sce_all_filt$EXP_celltype)] <- '#FF7F00' #orange
# BAL_colour_list[grep('NK', sce_all_filt$EXP_celltype)] <- '#FDBF6F' 
# # unknown clusters are colored with brown
# BAL_colour_list[sapply(BAL_colour_list, is.null)] <- '#B15928' #brown
# sce_all_filt$colour  <- BAL_colour_list

colData(sce_filt)[,c('named_cluster', 'long_named_cluster', 'verbose_cluster', 'EXP_celltype')] <- colData(sce_all_filt)[, c('named_cluster', 'long_named_cluster', 'verbose_cluster', 'EXP_celltype')]

```

And make some UMAPs for visulization.

```{r plot UMAPs to show annotated cell types of clusters, fig.width= 12, fig.height=8}
output_color_code_in_UMAP <- function(x = p1,
                                      clusterID_or_celltype = "named_cluster") 
{
  # Build the ggplot object
  p1_build <- ggplot_build(p1)
  
  # Extract color and label (named_cluster) from the data
  df_colors <- p1_build$data[[1]][, c("colour", "group")]
  
  # Pull named_cluster info from the input data used in plotUMAP
  # Assuming `sce_filt` is a SingleCellExperiment object
  if(clusterID_or_celltype == "named_cluster")
  {
    named_cluster_vec <- sce_filt$named_cluster 
  }else if(clusterID_or_celltype == "cluster")
  {
    named_cluster_vec <- sce_filt$cluster
  }else if(clusterID_or_celltype == "long_named_cluster")
  {
    named_cluster_vec <- sce_filt$long_named_cluster
  }
  
  
  # Create a data frame with unique mapping between factor value and actual value
  df_mapping <- data.frame(
    named_cluster = named_cluster_vec,
    group = as.numeric(factor(named_cluster_vec))
  )
  
  # Remove duplicates to get one row per cluster
  df_mapping_unique <- unique(df_mapping)
  
  # Merge with extracted color info

  color_cluster_map <- df_colors %>%
    distinct(group, colour) %>%
    inner_join(df_mapping_unique, by = "group") %>%
    select(named_cluster, colour)
  # distinct(group, colour) Keeps only unique combinations of group and colour
  # View result
  # print(color_cluster_map)
  # Define your custom color palette (names must match your 'cell_type' exactly)
  
  # color_cluster_map <- base:::merge(color_cluster_map, unique(dat_annot[ , c("cluster_id", "cell_type")]) ,
  #                    by.x = "named_cluster", by.y = "cluster_id")
  # 
  # color_cluster_map$named_cluster <- paste(color_cluster_map$named_cluster,
  #                                          color_cluster_map$cell_type,
  #                                          sep = "_")
  # dat_annot$named_cluster <- paste(dat_annot$cluster_id, dat_annot$cell_type,
  #                                  sep = "_")
  
  cell_type_colors <- setNames(
    as.character(color_cluster_map[["colour"]]),  # values
    as.character(color_cluster_map[["named_cluster"]])   # names
  )
  return(cell_type_colors)
  
}

selected_point_size = 0.2
# plot automatic assigned color for each cell type and cluster in UMAP
sce_filt
p1 <- plotUMAP(sce_filt, colour_by="cluster", text_by = "cluster",  point_size = selected_point_size) + 
  guides(colour = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size=15, alpha = 1))) + 
  theme(text = element_text(size = 20)) + ggtitle(paste('UMAP', sampSet))
print(p1)
clusterid_colors <- output_color_code_in_UMAP (p1, "cluster")
print(clusterid_colors)  

p1 <- plotUMAP(sce_filt, colour_by = "named_cluster", text_by = "cluster", point_size = selected_point_size) +
  guides(colour = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 15, alpha = 1))) +
  ggtitle(paste('UMAP', sampSet))

print(p1)
cell_type_colors <- output_color_code_in_UMAP (p1, "named_cluster")
print(cell_type_colors)


p1 <- plotUMAP(sce_filt, colour_by = "long_named_cluster", text_by = "cluster", point_size = selected_point_size) +
  guides(colour = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 15, alpha = 1))) +
  ggtitle(paste('UMAP', sampSet))

print(p1)



p1 <- plotUMAP(sce_filt, colour_by = "named_cluster", text_by = "named_cluster", point_size = selected_point_size) + 
  guides(colour = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 15, alpha = 1), title = 'Cell type')) + 
  ggtitle(paste('UMAP', sampSet)) 
print(p1)  

# draw additional UMAP with cluster 11, 12 not been displayed for subset_adaptive_immune_cells requested by collaborators
if(bsp_num == "bsp_248" )
{
  if(sampSet == "subset_adaptive_immune_cells")
  {
    sce_tem  <- sce_filt[, sce_filt$cluster != "11"]  
    sce_tem  <- sce_tem[, sce_tem$cluster != "12"]    
  }else if(sampSet == "subset_non_immune_cells")
  {
    sce_tem  <- sce_filt[, sce_filt$cluster != "22"]
  }else
  {
    sce_tem  <- sce_filt
  }


  p1 <- plotUMAP(sce_tem, colour_by = "named_cluster", text_by = "cluster", point_size = selected_point_size) +
  guides(colour = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 15, alpha = 1))) +
  ggtitle(paste('UMAP', sampSet))
  print(p1)  
  p1 <- plotUMAP(sce_tem, colour_by = "long_named_cluster", text_by = "cluster", point_size = selected_point_size) +
  guides(colour = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 15, alpha = 1))) +
  ggtitle(paste('UMAP', sampSet))
  print(p1)    
}



```

```

```


add a heatmap of all defining gene markers for our cell types
```{r add a heatmap of all defining gene markers for our cell types}
# # install.packages('gplots')
# library(gplots)
# library(viridis)
# 
# defining_genes <- c("CD163", "FLT3", "CD14", "CD4", "CADM1", "CSF1R", "FCGR1A", "BCL6", "CCL17", "IRF8", "ADAM8", "PRDM1", "IRF4", "JCHAIN" , "TNFRSF17", "TNFRSF13B", "CD19", "PAX5", "EBF1", "MS4A1", "CD79B", "IRF8", "CD28", "CD5", "CD3D", "CD3E", "CD8A", "CD8B", "FOXP3", "E2F1", "E2F2", "KLRK1", "KLRB1", "KIT", "NCR1", "TBX21", "EOMES", "GATA3", "IL1RL1", "IL7R", "ID3", "ACTG1", "GPR37", "NAPSA", "SFTPA1", "RPS3A", "RPS26", "RPS11", "RPL37")

# names(rowData(defining_sce))[which(names(rowData(defining_sce))=="start")]="gene_start"

```


add a bubbleplot of defining genes

# #reorder axis's using heatmap dendrogram
